executive_summary: Build workflow execution failed due to missing or inaccessible GitHub Actions workflow file. The workflow dispatch endpoint returned HTTP 404, indicating that the .github/workflows/build.yml file does not exist in the repository or the GitHub token lacks necessary permissions to access it. This is a critical configuration error that prevents any build execution from occurring.

errors.title: GitHub Actions Workflow File Not Found
errors.description: The GitHub Actions workflow file (.github/workflows/build.yml) is missing from the repository or inaccessible. The workflow dispatch API endpoint returned HTTP 404 error when attempting to trigger the build workflow.
errors.error_message: HTTP Error 404: Not Found. Workflow dispatch endpoint returned 404. Possible causes: token missing workflow/actions permissions, workflow not accessible on this repo/branch, or Actions disabled. dispatch_url=https://api.github.com/repos/loginkarun/scib_demo/actions/workflows/.github%2Fworkflows%2Fbuild.yml/dispatches response_body={"message":"Not Found","documentation_url":"https://docs.github.com/rest/actions/workflows#create-a-workflow-dispatch-event","status":"404"}
errors.root_cause: The workflow file .github/workflows/build.yml does not exist in the repository at the specified branch (main), or the GitHub Personal Access Token (PAT) used for authentication lacks the required 'workflow' or 'actions' scope permissions to access and dispatch workflows.
errors.impact: Complete build pipeline failure. No builds can be executed until the workflow file is created or permissions are corrected. This blocks all CI/CD automation, testing, and deployment processes.

automated_remediation: Create .github/workflows/build.yml file with standard Maven build configuration
Verify GitHub token has 'workflow' and 'actions' scopes enabled
Ensure GitHub Actions is enabled for the repository
Validate workflow file syntax using GitHub Actions validator
manual_steps: 1. Navigate to repository settings and verify GitHub Actions is enabled
2. Check GitHub token permissions at https://github.com/settings/tokens
4. Create .github/workflows directory if it doesn't exist
5. Add build.yml workflow file with proper Maven build steps
6. Commit and push the workflow file to the main branch
7. Verify workflow appears in Actions tab of repository
recommended_fix.sample_workflow_file:
name: Maven Build

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
  push:
    branches: [ main ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main ]

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v3
      - name: Set up JDK 11
        uses: actions/setup-java@v3
        with:
          java-version: '11'
          distribution: 'temurin'
      - name: Build with Maven
        run: mvn clean install
      - name: Run tests
        run: mvn test

errors.title: GitHub API Authentication or Permission Issue
errors.description: The GitHub Personal Access Token may lack the necessary permissions to dispatch workflows, or the token may have expired or been revoked.
errors.error_message: Workflow dispatch endpoint returned 404. Possible causes: token missing workflow/actions permissions, workflow not accessible on this repo/branch, or Actions disabled.
errors.root_cause: The GitHub PAT token may not have the 'workflow' scope enabled, which is required to trigger workflow_dispatch events. Alternatively, the token may have expired or been revoked.
errors.impact: Unable to programmatically trigger GitHub Actions workflows. This prevents automated build triggers and CI/CD pipeline execution.

automated_remediation: Validate token permissions via GitHub API
Generate new token with correct scopes if current token is invalid
Update token in environment variables or secrets management system
manual_steps: 1. Go to GitHub Settings > Developer settings > Personal access tokens
2. Check if the configured token exists and is active
3. Verify token has the following scopes enabled: 'repo', 'workflow', 'actions'
4. If token is missing scopes, regenerate token with correct permissions
5. Update token in CI/CD configuration and environment variables
6. Test token by making a test API call to GitHub Actions API

errors.title: GitHub Actions Disabled for Repository
errors.description: GitHub Actions may be disabled at the repository or organization level, preventing workflow execution.
errors.error_message: Possible causes: token missing workflow/actions permissions, workflow not accessible on this repo/branch, or Actions disabled.
errors.root_cause: GitHub Actions feature may be disabled in the repository settings, or organization policies may restrict Actions usage.
errors.impact: No workflows can be executed until Actions is enabled. This affects all CI/CD automation for the repository.

automated_remediation: Check repository settings via GitHub API
Enable Actions if disabled (requires admin permissions)
manual_steps: 2. Click on 'Actions' in the left sidebar
3. Under 'Actions permissions', ensure 'Allow all actions and reusable workflows' is selected
4. If using organization-level policies, check organization settings
5. Save changes and retry workflow dispatch

blocking_issues: Missing workflow file prevents any build execution
No action_url available for log analysis

compliance_notes.audit_trail: Error classification performed on build attempt 3. No GitHub Actions run was executed due to missing workflow file. All three attempts (1, 2, 3) failed with identical 404 errors.
compliance_notes.remediation_priority: IMMEDIATE - Critical blocker preventing all build operations
compliance_notes.estimated_resolution_time: 15-30 minutes (workflow file creation and token verification)
compliance_notes.automation_readiness: Partial - Can automate workflow file creation, but token permission changes require manual GitHub settings access

next_steps: 1. Create .github/workflows/build.yml file with Maven build configuration
2. Verify GitHub token has 'workflow' scope enabled
3. Confirm GitHub Actions is enabled for repository
4. Commit workflow file to main branch
6. Monitor for successful workflow dispatch and execution

metadata.classification_engine: DevOps Automation Agent - Build Error Classifier
metadata.analysis_method: Log-based error pattern matching and GitHub API response analysis
